title: perf: reduce Tinybird vCPU pressure on developer earnings
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
Reduces Tinybird vCPU-budget pressure behind intermittent 429s on account endpoints (a peak minute hit 246s vs the 180s/min budget, surfacing to users as 502s).

- Rewrite the `developer_earnings` pipe to a single `GROUPING SETS` pass instead of scanning `dev_events` 3×, cutting rows read ~3× (~795M → ~265M per call on a 90-day window). Output is byte-identical — validated against prod data across multiple devs (day/hour grain) and already deployed to both Tinybird workspaces.
- Map Tinybird 429s (vCPU budget) to a typed error so `/balance/today` degrades to empty earnings instead of leaking a raw 502.
- Cache `/balance/today` for 60s, matching the sibling usage/earnings endpoints — it was the only one uncached.

gist.headline: Balancing the books faster
gist.blurb: We've optimized how developer earnings are calculated under the hood. Your balance checks are now faster, cached, and much less likely to hit a snag during peak traffic.
gist.summary: Optimized the Tinybird developer_earnings query using GROUPING SETS to cut rows read by 3x, added 60s caching to /balance/today, and implemented graceful degradation for vCPU limit errors.
gist.impact: Developer account endpoints are now significantly faster and more reliable, gracefully returning empty earnings instead of 502 errors during peak database load.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as an accountant, organizing gold coins efficiently with a tiny bird (representing Tinybird). Focus on speed, math, and smooth operations.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny green accountant visor, happily organizing shiny gold pixel coins alongside a small blue pixel bird. A retro computer monitor in the background displays a clean, fast-moving green line graph. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and warm peach gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
